title: Bookstore Platform
description: An online bookstore platform with a catalog, cart, and checkout, exercising the full OpenSpec v1.1 surface.
background: The business is moving from a third-party storefront to an owned platform to control the catalog, pricing, and checkout experience.

goals.title: Let shoppers find books fast
goals.description: Provide search and browse over the catalog with low latency.
successCriteria: A shopper can find a book by title or author in under one second

goals.title: Convert carts to orders
goals.description: Offer a reliable checkout that captures payment and creates an order.
successCriteria: A valid cart can be checked out into a paid order

goals.title: Stay resilient under load
goals.description: Keep the storefront responsive during traffic spikes.
successCriteria: The storefront stays within latency targets at peak load

goals.title: Be operable in production
goals.description: Expose health, metrics, and logs for on-call operators.
successCriteria: Operators can diagnose a failing request from telemetry alone

requirements.title: Browse the catalog
requirements.description: Shoppers can search and page through the book catalog.

acceptanceCriteria.then: matching books are returned, paged and ranked by relevance

constraints: Search must return within the latency NFR target

requirements.title: Manage a cart
requirements.description: Shoppers can add, update, and remove cart items.

acceptanceCriteria.when: they add a book to the cart
acceptanceCriteria.then: the cart reflects the new item and updated total

requirements.title: Check out a cart
requirements.description: Shoppers can convert a cart into a paid order.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a non-empty cart and valid payment details
acceptanceCriteria.then: payment is captured and an order is created

architecture: A modular monolith split into catalog, cart, and checkout modules behind an API gateway. State lives in PostgreSQL; search is served from a read-optimized index; payments go through an external provider.

assumptions: A single payment provider is integrated at launch
Catalog data is imported from the legacy storefront

folderStructures.description: Module layout of the modular monolith.
folderStructures.content:
src/
  catalog/
  cart/
  checkout/
  gateway/
  shared/

sharedPatterns.name: Error handling
sharedPatterns.description: All modules surface errors as typed Result objects rather than thrown exceptions at boundaries.

codeStandards.errorHandling: Return Result<T, AppError> at module boundaries
codeStandards.naming: camelCase for values, PascalCase for types

acceptanceCriteria.when: a shopper completes a browse-to-checkout journey
acceptanceCriteria.then: each step satisfies its requirement's acceptance criteria

nonFunctionalRequirements.description: Catalog search stays fast under load.
Payment handling avoids storing raw card data.

guardrails.description: Raw card numbers must never touch platform storage or logs.
guardrails.consequence: Storing PANs would pull the whole platform into PCI scope and create breach liability.

guardrails.description: Payment capture must be idempotent per order.
guardrails.rationale: Checkout can be retried by clients.

epics.title: Platform foundation
epics.description: Set up the gateway, shared modules, persistence, and telemetry.
epics.objective: A deployable skeleton with persistence, gateway routing, and telemetry wired in.

sharedPatterns.name: Error handling
sharedPatterns.description: Reuse the typed Result boundary pattern across foundation modules.

tickets.title: Set up the API gateway
tickets.description: Add the gateway with routing and telemetry middleware.

acceptanceCriteria.then: it is routed to the right module with a trace span

implementationSteps.text: Add the router and module mounting

guardrails: No request body is logged in full

typeSnippets.content: export type Handler = (req: Request) => Promise<Result<Response, AppError>>

tickets.title: Add database migrations
tickets.description: Create the initial schema for books, carts, and orders.

acceptanceCriteria.then: the books, carts, and orders tables exist

codeSnippets.reason: Migrations are plain SQL files; no in-spec code snippet adds value here.

epics.title: Cart and checkout
epics.description: Implement cart management and the checkout flow with payment capture.
epics.objective: A shopper can build a cart and complete a paid checkout.

apiContracts.name: Checkout API
apiContracts.description: POST /checkout captures payment and creates an order.

tickets.title: Implement cart endpoints
tickets.description: Add, update, and remove cart items.

tickets.title: Implement checkout and payment capture
tickets.description: Convert a cart into a paid order via the payment provider.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a non-empty cart and valid payment
acceptanceCriteria.then: payment is captured idempotently and an order is created

implementationSteps.text: Validate the cart and payment details

guardrails: Capture must be idempotent per order

blueprints.title: System architecture
blueprints.description: Modules behind the gateway and their data stores.
blueprints.content:
flowchart TB
  gw[API Gateway] --> catalog
  gw --> cart
  gw --> checkout
  checkout --> pay[Payment Provider]

blueprints.title: Core data model
blueprints.content:
erDiagram
  BOOK ||--o{ CART_ITEM : referenced_by
  CART ||--o{ CART_ITEM : contains
  CART ||--o| ORDER : becomes

blueprints.title: Checkout sequence
blueprints.content:
sequenceDiagram
  Shopper->>Checkout: POST /checkout
  Checkout->>Payment: capture(idempotencyKey)
  Payment-->>Checkout: captured
  Checkout->>DB: create order
  Checkout-->>Shopper: 201 Order

blueprints.title: ADR: modular monolith over microservices
blueprints.description: Why we start as a modular monolith.
blueprints.content:
# Context
Launch needs speed and low operational overhead.
# Decision
Ship a modular monolith with clear module boundaries.
# Consequences
Lower ops cost now; modules can be extracted later if needed.
